headline: Sijil Kemahiran Malaysia Holders Earn 40% Less Than Degree Holders
context: Skills certificate holders face persistent wage discrimination despite equivalent competency in technical fields.

cards.big: SKM Level 4 holders earn average RM2,100 versus RM3,500 for degree holders in same fields
cards.sub: Wage gap persists even when job functions are identical between qualifications

cards.big: Malaysian Qualifications Framework places SKM and degrees at equivalent levels since 2007
cards.sub: Formal equivalence has not translated to employer recognition or pay parity

cards.big: 74% of employers in MIHRM 2025 survey require degrees for jobs performable by SKM holders
cards.sub: Credentialism rather than competency drives hiring practices

cards.big: Switzerland's vocational diploma holders earn 92% of university graduate salaries on average
cards.sub: Strong industry-certification linkage ensures skills-based pay not credential-based

cards.big: Devaluing skills certificates while promoting TVET sends contradictory signals to young people
Should government mandate equal pay for equivalent MQF-level qualifications in public sector
